summary: A pastebin-style application with file/image upload support, paste expiration, and secure data handling. Fixing expiration bypass on downloads and ensuring backend truly blocks access to attachments after expiration.
architectural_notes: Paste expiration must be enforced server-side at time of each request, not just on page load
Expired paste attachments/images must be deleted or refused at the backend, not just hidden in the UI
known_issues: Download bypass: expiration check only on page load allows downloads after paste expires if page stays open

existing_features.title: On the frontend, in PasteViewPage.tsx, before triggering a file or image download, perform a fresh expiration check against the backend (re-fetch or re-validate the paste) at the moment the user clicks the download button. If the paste is found to be expired at that point, abort the download and display the expired error state instead of proceeding with the download.

new_feature_requests.summary: Real-time expiration check at download time (not just on page load)
Backend enforcement to block/delete attachments and images after paste expiration

On the backend, when a request is made to retrieve or download an attachment or image blob, check whether the parent paste has expired before returning any data. If the paste is expired, refuse to serve the attachment and return an error (e.g., #expired or equivalent variant). Do not rely solely on UI-side expiration gating.

projectName: Fix expiration bypass: enforce real-time expiration checks on downloads and block expired attachments on backend
